Package org.rodinp.core

Class RodinCore

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.core.runtime.Plugin
org.rodinp.core.RodinCore
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.osgi.framework.BundleActivator
public class RodinCore extends org.eclipse.core.runtime.Plugin
The plug-in runtime class for the Rodin core plug-in containing the core (UI-free) support for Rodin projects.

Like all plug-in runtime classes (subclasses of Plugin), this class is automatically instantiated by the platform when the plug-in gets activated. Clients must not attempt to instantiate plug-in runtime classes directly.

The single instance of this class can be accessed from any plug-in declaring the Rodin core plug-in as a prerequisite via RodinCore.getRodinCore(). The Rodin core plug-in will be activated automatically if not already active.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • addElementChangedListener

      public static void addElementChangedListener(IElementChangedListener listener)
      Adds the given listener for changes to Rodin elements. Has no effect if an identical listener is already registered. This listener will only be notified during the POST_CHANGE resource change notification and any reconcile operation (POST_RECONCILE). For finer control of the notification, use addElementChangedListener(IElementChangedListener,int), which allows to specify a different eventMask.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener
      See Also:
      • ElementChangedEvent

    • addElementChangedListener

      public static void addElementChangedListener(IElementChangedListener listener, int eventMask)
      Adds the given listener for changes to Rodin elements. Has no effect if an identical listener is already registered. After completion of this method, the given listener will be registered for exactly the specified events. If they were previously registered for other events, they will be deregistered.

      Once registered, a listener starts receiving notification of changes to Rodin elements in the model. The listener continues to receive notifications until it is replaced or removed.

      Listeners can listen for several types of event as defined in ElementChangeEvent. Clients are free to register for any number of event types however if they register for more than one, it is their responsibility to ensure they correctly handle the case where the same java element change shows up in multiple notifications. Clients are guaranteed to receive only the events for which they are registered.

      Parameters:
      listener - the listener
      eventMask - the bit-wise OR of all event types of interest to the listener
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • IElementChangedListener
      • ElementChangedEvent
      • removeElementChangedListener(IElementChangedListener)

      public static <T extends IInternalElement> IInternalElementType<T> getInternalElementType(String id)

    • removeElementChangedListener

      public static void removeElementChangedListener(IElementChangedListener listener)
      Removes the given element changed listener. Has no affect if an identical listener is not registered.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener

    • run

      public static void run(org.eclipse.core.resources.IWorkspaceRunnable action, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Runs the given action as an atomic Rodin database operation.

      After running a method that modifies Rodin elements, registered listeners receive after-the-fact notification of what just transpired, in the form of an element changed event. This method allows clients to call a number of methods that modify Rodin elements and only have element changed event notifications reported at the end of the entire batch.

      If this method is called outside the dynamic scope of another such call, this method runs the action and then reports a single element changed event describing the net effect of all changes done to Rodin elements by the action.

      If this method is called in the dynamic scope of another such call, this method simply runs the action.

      Parameters:
      action - the action to perform
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the operation failed.

    • run

      public static void run(org.eclipse.core.resources.IWorkspaceRunnable action, org.eclipse.core.runtime.jobs.ISchedulingRule rule, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Runs the given action as an atomic Rodin database operation.

      After running a method that modifies Rodin elements, registered listeners receive after-the-fact notification of what just transpired, in the form of an element changed event. This method allows clients to call a number of methods that modify Rodin elements and only have element changed event notifications reported at the end of the entire batch.

      If this method is called outside the dynamic scope of another such call, this method runs the action and then reports a single element changed event describing the net effect of all changes done to Rodin elements by the action.

      If this method is called in the dynamic scope of another such call, this method simply runs the action.

      The supplied scheduling rule is used to determine whether this operation can be run simultaneously with workspace changes in other threads. See IWorkspace.run(...) for more details.

      Parameters:
      action - the action to perform
      rule - the scheduling rule to use when running this operation, or null if there are no scheduling restrictions for this operation.
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the operation failed.

    • start

      public void start(org.osgi.framework.BundleContext context) throws Exception
      Specified by:

      Overrides:
      start in class org.eclipse.core.runtime.Plugin
      Throws:

    • stop

      public void stop(org.osgi.framework.BundleContext context) throws Exception
      Specified by:
      stop in interface org.osgi.framework.BundleActivator
      Overrides:
      stop in class org.eclipse.core.runtime.Plugin
      Throws:
      Exception

    • asRodinElement

      public static IRodinElement asRodinElement(Object object)
      Returns the given object as a Rodin element if possible, null otherwise.

      A non-null value is returned iff the given object is a Rodin element or adaptable to a Rodin element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      object - the object to adapt to a Rodin element
      Returns:
      the given object as a Rodin element or null

    • valueOf

      public static IRodinElement valueOf(String handleIdentifier)
      Returns the Rodin element corresponding to the given handle identifier generated by IRodinElement.getHandleIdentifier(), or null if unable to create the associated element handle.

      Since Rodin 3.0, null is also returned when the returned handle would violate the database restrictions (see the org.rodinp.core.itemRelations extension point).

      Parameters:
      handleIdentifier - the given handle identifier
      Returns:
      the Rodin element corresponding to the handle identifier, or null if the identifier can't be parsed

    • getRefinementManager

      public static IRefinementManager getRefinementManager()
      Returns the refinement manager instance.
      Returns:
      the refinement manager
      Since:
      1.4
      See Also:
      • IRefinementManager

    • valueOf

      public static IRodinFile valueOf(org.eclipse.core.resources.IFile file)
      Returns the Rodin file element corresponding to the given file, or null if unable to associate the given file with a Rodin element.

      Creating a Rodin element has the side effect of creating and opening all of the element's parents if they are not yet open.

      Parameters:
      file - the given file
      Returns:
      the Rodin file element corresponding to the given file, or null if unable to associate the given file with a Rodin file element

    • valueOf

      public static IRodinProject valueOf(org.eclipse.core.resources.IProject project)
      Returns the Rodin project corresponding to the given project.

      Calling this method has the side effect of creating and opening all of the project's parents if they are not yet open.

      Parameters:
      project - the given project
      Returns:
      the Rodin project corresponding to the given project, or null if the given project is null

    • valueOf

      public static IRodinElement valueOf(org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource resource)
      Returns the Rodin element corresponding to the given resource, or null if unable to associate the given resource with a Rodin element.

      The resource must be one of:

      • a project - the element returned is the corresponding IRodinProject
      • a Rodin file - the element returned is the corresponding RodinFile
      • the workspace root resource - the element returned is the IRodinDB

      Calling this method has the side effect of creating and opening all of the element's parents if they are not yet open.

      Parameters:
      resource - the given resource
      Returns:
      the Rodin element corresponding to the given resource, or null if unable to associate the given resource with a Rodin element

    • valueOf

      public static IRodinDB valueOf(org.eclipse.core.resources.IWorkspaceRoot root)
      Returns the Rodin database.
      Parameters:
      root - the given root
      Returns:
      the Rodin database, or null if the root is null

    • convert

      public static IConversionResult convert(IRodinProject project, boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Convert a Rodin project to a new version. This method does not manipulate the project but only computes what is necessary for the conversion to be done. This is returned as a result (IConversionResult).
      Parameters:
      project - the Rodin project to be converted
      force - whether or not files are expected to be in synchrony
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Returns:
      the proposed conversion in an IConversionResult
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem creating a conversion

    • getRodinDB

      public static IRodinDB getRodinDB()
      Returns the Rodin database element.
      Returns:
      the Rodin database

    • makeIndexQuery

      public static IIndexQuery makeIndexQuery()
      Returns a fresh object that allows querying the indexer database.
      Returns:
      a fresh query object
      See Also:
      • IIndexQuery

    • getOccurrenceKind

      public static IOccurrenceKind getOccurrenceKind(String id)
      Returns the occurrence kind declared with the given id in an extension of the extension point org.rodinp.core.occurrenceKinds. Returns null if no extension declares the given id.
      Parameters:
      id - the occurrence kind identifier
      Returns:
      the occurrence kind of the given id or null if there is none

    • getRodinLocation

      public static IRodinLocation getRodinLocation(IInternalElement element)
      Returns the location pointing at the given element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      element - a Rodin element
      Returns:
      the location pointing at the given element

    • getInternalLocation

      public static IInternalLocation getInternalLocation(IInternalElement element)
      Returns the location pointing at the given internal element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      element - a Rodin internal element
      Returns:
      the location pointing at the given element

    • getInternalLocation

      public static IAttributeLocation getInternalLocation(IInternalElement element, IAttributeType attributeType)
      Returns the location pointing at the attribute of the given type in the given internal element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      element - a Rodin internal element
      attributeType - the type of the attribute to point at
      Returns:
      the location pointing at the given attribute of the given element

    • getInternalLocation

      public static IAttributeSubstringLocation getInternalLocation(IInternalElement element, IAttributeType.String attributeType, int start, int end)
      Returns the location pointing at the specified substring of the attribute of the given type in the given internal element. The substring is specified by giving its start and end positions.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Parameters:
      element - a Rodin internal element
      attributeType - the type of the attribute to point at
      start - the start position of the substring in the attribute value, that is the index of the first character. Must be non-negative and less than end
      end - the end position of the substring in the attribute value, that is the index of the last character plus one. Must be greater than start
      Returns:
      the location pointing at the specified substring of the given attribute of the given element
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the constraints on start and end are not fulfilled
